tasks.title: Implement structured telemetry event input and environment context
tasks.description:
Context: OpenSpec change enrich-failure-telemetry, tasks 1.1, 1.2, 1.4, and the telemetry-side parts of 1.5. Current telemetry.CaptureFailure accepts only tags, and Sentry can populate host-derived server_name unless explicitly neutralized.

Outcome: internal/telemetry has a structured failure/event input that separates scalar tags from context blocks; Sink, NoopSink, SentrySink, and existing telemetry test mocks use it; an environment-context builder returns rally version, go_os, go_arch, and term; Sentry events never carry host-derived server_name.

Files & scope: internal/telemetry/sink.go, noop.go, sentry.go, init/context-related files, tags.go only as needed, telemetry tests, and command/relay mocks that must compile after the interface change. Do not wire relay call-site state yet beyond mechanical signature updates needed for buildability.

Design: Keep filterable values as tags and nested/process context as contexts. Use internal/buildinfo for version, runtime.GOOS/GOARCH, and golang.org/x/term.IsTerminal(int(os.Stdout.Fd())) with $TERM or non-tty for terminal description. Neutralize Sentry server_name by setting a static non-host ClientOptions.ServerName or clearing event.ServerName in scrubEvent, with a test proving the outgoing event is not host-derived.

Acceptance: Targeted telemetry tests cover environment context fields, absence of hostname/username, tag/context separation, Noop behavior, and server_name neutralization. `go test ./internal/telemetry/... ./cmd/rally/... ./internal/relay/...` is green. Do not check off OpenSpec tasks.md boxes.

tasks.title: Add product DSN fallback and release injection
tasks.description:
Context: OpenSpec tasks 2.1-2.5. Rally currently resolves telemetry DSN from RALLY_TELEMETRY, SENTRY_DSN, and config only; release binaries need a baked default while preserving overrides.

Outcome: cmd/rally/main.go defines `DefaultSentryDSN = ""`, telemetry config loading passes it as the fallback DSN, GoReleaser injects it with `-X main.DefaultSentryDSN={{ .Env.RALLY_SENTRY_DSN }}`, and the release workflow exposes the `RALLY_SENTRY_DSN` secret only to GoReleaser.

Files & scope: cmd/rally/main.go, internal/telemetry/init.go and tests, .goreleaser.yaml, .github/workflows/release.yml. Keep the existing Version ldflag intact.

Design: Preserve DSN precedence exactly: RALLY_TELEMETRY=0 disables all telemetry; SENTRY_DSN overrides config and default; config telemetry.sentry_dsn overrides the baked default; baked default is used only when env/config are empty; empty effective DSN returns NoopSink.

Acceptance: Tests cover env beats config/default, config beats default, baked default activates telemetry when env/config are empty, and RALLY_TELEMETRY=0 disables even with env/config/default set and creates no telemetry side-effect files. `go test ./internal/telemetry/... ./cmd/rally/...` and `go build ./...` are green. Do not check off OpenSpec tasks.md boxes.

tasks.title: Add telemetry data dir and anonymous machine identity
tasks.description:
Context: OpenSpec tasks 3.1-3.4. The DSN lap gives telemetry a default activation path; active telemetry now needs an anonymous stable machine-local identifier without writing anything when telemetry is disabled.

Outcome: telemetry.Config includes DataDir, cmd/rally/main.go passes the resolved data dir into telemetry.Init before sink creation, and internal/telemetry has a helper that reads or creates `<dataDir>/machine-id` as a random 128-bit hex value with 0600 permissions. Disabled telemetry writes no file; unreadable/unwritable/no-data-dir cases fall back to an ephemeral per-process anonymous value.

Files & scope: internal/telemetry/, cmd/rally/main.go, and tests. Do not derive the value from hostname, username, MAC, repo path, or any machine attribute.

Design: Only resolve/create machine identity after the effective DSN says telemetry is active. Generate with crypto/rand, hex-encode, persist once, and never fail CLI startup because identity storage failed. Keep the helper small and testable so later relay-guid work can consume it without duplicating file handling.

Acceptance: Tests verify stable reads, absent file created once with private permissions, generated values are random-format and not machine-derived, disabled telemetry writes no file, and no data dir/unwritable data dir uses an ephemeral identity without failing. `go test ./internal/telemetry/... ./cmd/rally/...` is green. Do not check off OpenSpec tasks.md boxes.

tasks.title: Verify telemetry foundation, DSN activation, and machine identity
tasks.description:
Use the openspec-verify-change skill against enrich-failure-telemetry, then focus on OpenSpec tasks 1.1, 1.2, 1.4, 1.5, 2.1-2.5, and 3.1-3.4.

Before diffing, identify the intended merge target from the user, PR metadata, repo docs, branch config, or recent history; use that target in diff commands. Identify the first lap/try in this batch and treat older branch work as pre-existing unless the user asks to include it. Do not rewrite git history.

Trace the actual implementation, not just tests: confirm CaptureFailure now separates tags from contexts; environment context contains version/os/arch/term and no host identity; Sentry server_name is neutralized; DSN precedence matches the spec exactly including kill switch behavior; release ldflags preserve Version and inject DefaultSentryDSN; machine-id creation happens only for active telemetry and falls back ephemerally on storage failure.

Acceptance: Check off only the tasks in this slice that are verified correct and sufficiently thorough. Run the targeted tests for telemetry/cmd plus `go build ./...`. Fix only tiny safe one-liners inline; larger gaps become focused head laps with `laps add head`.

tasks.title: Harden home-prefix collapse in telemetry scrubbing
tasks.description:
Context: OpenSpec tasks 5.1, 5.3, and privacy portions of 5.4. The current scrubber drops sensitive keys and truncates values; this slice adds username stripping for home paths everywhere telemetry might transmit strings.

Outcome: internal/telemetry/scrubber.go has a home-prefix collapse helper using os.UserHomeDir(), and scrubEvent applies it recursively to string values in event contexts, breadcrumbs, span data, and free text such as raw provider signals/messages.

Files & scope: internal/telemetry/scrubber.go and scrubber tests. Keep non-home absolute paths unchanged unless they match an existing sensitive-key rule. Do not add cwd or relay identity fields in this lap; only provide the scrubber/privacy primitive.

Design: Collapse `/home/<user>/...` or the platform home path to `~/...`, including when embedded inside longer strings. Preserve existing sensitive-key redaction and size limiting. The helper should be deterministic and easy for later context-building code to reuse.

Acceptance: Tests cover direct cwd-style values, embedded home paths in raw text, username absence after scrubbing, non-home absolute paths remaining intact, and existing prompt/transcript sensitive field behavior still passing. `go test ./internal/telemetry/...` is green. Do not check off OpenSpec tasks.md boxes.

tasks.title: Attach relay identity and rally context to spans and failures
tasks.description:
Context: OpenSpec tasks 1.3, 4.1-4.3, 5.2, and the identity/cwd parts of 5.4. This depends on the structured event input, environment context, machine identity, and home-collapse helper.

Outcome: relay spans and captured failures carry a `rally` context block with version/os/arch/term, full anonymous machine id, and home-collapsed cwd; tags include local relay_id, relay_guid, relay_started_at, repo, and machine_id_prefix in the specified locations. Full machine id is context-only, never a tag.

Files & scope: internal/telemetry/tags.go or a new context builder, internal/relay/runner.go call sites, telemetry tests, and relay telemetry tests. Reuse the existing repo key from the relay logging/tagging path.

Design: Compute `relay_guid = <machine-id-prefix>-<repo-key>-<YYYYMMDD>-<relay_id>` from the machine id, existing repo key, relay StartedAt, and local relay id. Attach the same rally context to the relay span and every CaptureFailure event through the structured API. Keep the local relay_id tag for compatibility with summary/try records.

Acceptance: Tests prove relay_guid is stable for one relay and differs across machine ids, repo keys, and dates; relay_id remains tagged; machine_id_prefix is tagged; full machine_id appears only in context; cwd under home is collapsed to `~/...`; relay span and failure event both receive the rally context. `go test ./internal/telemetry/... ./internal/relay/...` is green. Do not check off OpenSpec tasks.md boxes.

tasks.title: Verify identity, context placement, and privacy guarantees
tasks.description:
Use the openspec-verify-change skill against enrich-failure-telemetry, then focus on OpenSpec tasks 1.3, 4.1-4.3, 5.1-5.4, and any unchecked privacy coverage from task 1.5.

Before diffing, identify the intended merge target and the first lap/try in this batch. Treat earlier branch work as pre-existing. Do not rewrite git history; substantial cleanup or redesign becomes a focused head lap.

Trace the data path end to end: how the environment context is built, how machine id and cwd enter the rally context, where relay_guid and relay_started_at are computed, how relay spans receive context/data, how CaptureFailure receives context, and whether scrubEvent still defends against home paths embedded in arbitrary strings. Confirm high-cardinality fields follow the spec: machine_id_prefix tag only, full machine_id context only.

Acceptance: Check off verified tasks in this slice only after code inspection plus targeted tests. Run telemetry and relay tests relevant to identity/privacy. Tiny one-liner fixes only inline; larger gaps become head laps.

tasks.title: Add failure-state telemetry event helpers
tasks.description:
Context: OpenSpec tasks 6.1, 6.2, 6.7, and helper-level parts of 6.8. The previous change improve-error-categorisation has landed and exposes TryResult.Evidence, FailureCategory, quota scope/reset fields, and active/probation/frozen/benched vocabulary.

Outcome: telemetry has a small event/state helper or input shape that can attach attempt, max_attempts, failure_category, agent_state, quota_scope, reset_at/reset_after, and a `failure_evidence` context for limit categories without re-classifying failures.

Files & scope: internal/telemetry/ and minimal type plumbing needed by relay call sites. Do not wire every runner capture site in this lap unless it is needed to keep tests compiling; the next lap owns runner integration.

Design: Read category and reset data from upstream FailureEvidence/StrategyDecision data supplied by the caller. For usage_limit, short_rate_limit, and provider_overloaded, attach bounded RawSignal and Message as a context block, not tags. For non-limit categories, omit raw signal context entirely. Run all context strings through the scrubber, including home path collapse. Do not include prompt/transcript-looking fields.

Acceptance: Unit tests cover tag names/values for attempt/budget/category/state/reset fields, failure_evidence context presence for limit categories, absence for non-limit categories, scrubbed home paths inside raw signal/message, and no prompt/transcript payload attached. `go test ./internal/telemetry/...` is green. Do not check off OpenSpec tasks.md boxes.

tasks.title: Wire failure-state telemetry into runner capture sites
tasks.description:
Context: OpenSpec tasks 6.3-6.6 and runner-facing parts of 6.8. Failure captures currently happen for terminal try failures, unfinalized agents, and relay stalls; this lap wires the structured state from the real run path.

Outcome: terminal try failure captures carry attempt, max_attempts, failure_category, quota/reset evidence where present, and the selected runner's resilience state; unfinalized-agent captures carry failure_category=incomplete_finalization with run/runner/max-attempt/last-attempt context where available; relay stall/all-frozen captures carry agent_state=frozen and relay/global context while omitting try-only fields. Agent-class failures that are only spans/logs remain unaffected and do not become Issues.

Files & scope: internal/relay/runner.go, route/resilience helpers only where needed to read state, cmd/rally telemetry tests or relay tests. Do not re-classify inside telemetry; consume TryResult.Evidence and the StrategyDecision/failure-category already produced in runOne.

Design: Preserve current issue-worthy criteria except for enriching events that are already captured. Be explicit about site-specific fields: terminal try failures can use attempt and max attempts; unfinalized captures may use last known attempt if available; relay-level all-frozen has no try_id, attempt, reset evidence, or raw signal. Reuse active/probation/frozen/benched names verbatim.

Acceptance: Tests show a captured usage-limit failure includes attempt/budget/category/quota_scope/reset/state tags and raw signal context; unfinalized captures include incomplete_finalization; relay stall captures include frozen state and omit try-only fields; agent-class span/log-only failures remain span/log-only. `go test ./internal/relay/... ./cmd/rally/... ./internal/telemetry/...` is green. Do not check off OpenSpec tasks.md boxes.

tasks.title: Broaden regression coverage for failure evidence scrubbing
tasks.description:
Context: OpenSpec tasks 6.6 and 6.8 are high-risk because they combine routing state, failure classification, and privacy scrubbing. This lap is a focused test pass after the runner wiring.

Outcome: Additional regression tests cover the realistic event payloads emitted by terminal try failures, unfinalized-agent failures, all-frozen relay stalls, and limit-category raw-signal captures.

Files & scope: internal/relay/*test.go, internal/telemetry/*test.go, and cmd/rally telemetry tests if existing mocks need assertions. Avoid production changes unless a test exposes a tiny obvious fix; larger issues should become head laps.

Design: Use fixtures that exercise the real CaptureFailure inputs, not isolated map assembly only. Include a raw signal/message containing a home path and prompt/transcript-looking sensitive keys to prove scrubber defense-in-depth. Include a non-limit category to prove no raw-signal context is attached.

Acceptance: The test suite proves all task 6.6 and 6.8 scenarios and stays green for `go test ./internal/telemetry/... ./internal/relay/... ./cmd/rally/...`. Do not check off OpenSpec tasks.md boxes.

tasks.title: Verify failure-state and raw limit-signal telemetry
tasks.description:
Use the openspec-verify-change skill against enrich-failure-telemetry, then focus on OpenSpec tasks 6.1-6.8.

Before diffing, identify the intended merge target and first lap/try in this batch. Treat pre-batch work as pre-existing. Do not rewrite git history; substantive fixes become focused head laps.

Trace the exact values from the runner path: TryResult.Evidence and StrategyDecision into terminal CaptureFailure; resilience state lookup into agent_state; quota/reset evidence into scalar tags; RawSignal/Message into `failure_evidence` context only for usage_limit/short_rate_limit/provider_overloaded; unfinalized-agent classification; and all-frozen relay-level capture behavior. Confirm telemetry does not re-classify and does not turn agent-class span/log-only failures into Issues.

Acceptance: Check off task 6 boxes only after code inspection plus targeted tests. Run relevant relay and telemetry tests; include any failures or test gaps in the wrapup. Tiny one-liner fixes only inline.

tasks.title: Document enriched telemetry behavior and privacy controls
tasks.description:
Context: OpenSpec task 7.1. Implementation should already be in place; this lap documents the operator-facing behavior without introducing OpenSpec-specific language into generic Rally surfaces.

Outcome: User-facing docs describe baked default DSN behavior, env/config override precedence, RALLY_TELEMETRY=0 kill switch, new context fields, anonymous machine id storage and reset by deleting the file, machine_id_prefix tag vs full machine_id context placement, relay_guid, cwd home-prefix collapse, raw limit-signal privacy handling, and guarantees that hostname/username/IP are not sent.

Files & scope: README.md or the existing telemetry/privacy docs if present. Avoid changing code unless doc examples reveal a small naming mismatch.

Design: Keep the explanation concise and practical. Mention that Sentry DSNs are client ingestion endpoints, not privileged API secrets, but users can disable telemetry with RALLY_TELEMETRY=0. Explain exactly where `<dataDir>/machine-id` lives based on configured/default data dir.

Acceptance: Docs accurately match the implemented names and precedence. Any doc tests or markdown checks used by the repo are green. Do not check off OpenSpec tasks.md boxes.

tasks.title: Final full-change verification for enriched failure telemetry
tasks.description:
Run the complete openspec-verify-change skill for enrich-failure-telemetry and inspect the whole diff. Identify the intended merge target first and use it in diff commands; identify the first lap/try in this batch and treat earlier branch work as pre-existing unless the user asks otherwise. Do not rewrite git history.

Confirm all 30 OpenSpec tasks are done correctly and coherently: structured telemetry event input; environment context and server_name neutralization; DSN default precedence and release injection; active-only anonymous machine identity; relay_guid and machine identity placement; cwd/home stripping across contexts/breadcrumbs/span data/raw text; failure state and quota/reset tags; raw limit-signal context only for provider-limit categories; docs and privacy guarantees.

Review with depth, not just green tests: trace the core call sites in internal/telemetry, cmd/rally/main.go, release config, and internal/relay/runner.go. Confirm no hostname/username/IP or full machine id leaks into tags; no prompt/transcript content is sent; disabled telemetry creates no machine-id file; and agent-class failures recorded as spans/logs are unaffected.

Acceptance: Run the targeted suites plus full `go test ./...` and `go build ./...`. Check off any remaining tasks.md boxes only for verified-correct work. For substantive gaps, add focused head laps using additive/revert commits only; do not turn final verification into a hidden implementation phase.
